summary: Phase 8.A.2a (sandbox-verifiable JS implementation, foundation half — codec + verifier + CLI + REPLAY_DIGEST) approved. Per the established Phase 7.A.2a/7.A.2b precedent, the original Phase 8.A.2 scope (codec + verifier + router + save + main UI) was internally split into 8.A.2a (foundation: pure functions sandbox-verifiable) and 8.A.2b (wiring: src/router/, src/save/, src/main.ts diagnostic UI, deploy.yml budget bump). 8.A.2a lands the load-bearing byte-level contracts that 8.A.2b's wiring depends on. Concrete deliverables: (1) src/core/encode.ts gains decodeAction(bytes, offset) → { action, bytesConsumed } per memo addendum B2 with byte-explicit validation: ACTION_VERSION = 0x01 check, type_len ∈ [1, 64], strict UTF-8 type bytes, strictly-increasing tag ordering across {TAG_TARGET=0x10, TAG_ITEM=0x20, TAG_DIR=0x30}, unknown-tag REJECT (forces forward-compat through release pinning, NOT silent skips per addendum B2), TAG_TARGET 4-byte int32 LE, TAG_ITEM 1+N bytes (item_len ≤ 255), TAG_DIR 1 byte 0..7. All rejections produce pinned `decodeAction:`-prefixed error messages. (2) src/share/encode.ts: ACTION_LOG_MAGIC = [0x49 0x43 0x45] ("ICE"), ACTION_LOG_VERSION = 0x01, buildActionLogEnvelope(actions: readonly Action[]): Uint8Array (header layout `[ICE\x01][actionCount:u32 LE][concat(encodeAction)]`), encodeActionLog(actions): string returning `base64url(zlibSync(envelope, { level: 1 }))` per memo addendum B1. The zlibSync wire form (NOT deflateSync — lint-banned at the share scope) inherits Phase 4.B's cross-OS byte-identity guarantee. (3) src/share/decode.ts: parseActionLogEnvelope(bytes) inverts the envelope; decodeActionLog(wire): Action[] inverts the full base64url + unzlibSync + envelope-parse + decodeAction-loop pipeline. Pinned `action-log:`-prefixed error messages for: bad magic (`bad magic — expected 'ICE' (49 43 45), got '<hex>'`), unsupported version (`unsupported version <v> (this build supports version 1) — load a newer release with 'releases/<commit>/'`), truncated envelope, trailing bytes after final action, declared count mismatch, base64url-decode failure, zlib-decompress failure. (4) src/verifier/verify.ts: verify(args: VerifyArgs): VerifyResult discriminated union per memo decision 10 + advisory A4. VerifyResult kinds: valid, fingerprint-mismatch (returns recomputed truthful fp), ruleset-mismatch (expected/actual), atlas-mismatch (expected/actual; required field per A4), state-hash-mismatch (expected/actual), outcome-mismatch (expected/actual), log-rejected (with reason). Build-context-trust-anchored: reads commitHash/rulesetVersion/atlasBinaryHash from src/build-info.ts; the router's redirect path (8.A.2b) is responsible for ensuring the right build BEFORE verify() is called. Both 22-char short and 43-char full fingerprint claims supported. (5) tools/verify.ts: Node CLI wrapper that reads JSON VerifyArgs from stdin, calls verify(), writes JSON VerifyResult to stdout. Exit codes: 0 valid, 1 not-valid, 2 stdin-parse-error, 3 unexpected. (6) src/core/self-test.ts: REPLAY_DIGEST = WIN_DIGEST (by construction; the codec is identity on action sequences) + new `replay-cross-runtime-digest` self-test entry that exercises decodeActionLog(encodeActionLog(SELF_TEST_WIN_LOG)) → runScripted → assert outcome === "won" + state-hash equals REPLAY_DIGEST. Cross-runtime: any drift in zlibSync byte identity, magic+version+actionCount header layout, decodeAction tag-validation, base64url alphabet, surfaces here in any of Node/Chromium/Firefox/WebKit. (7) Tests: tests/core/decode-action.test.ts (25 tests including positive single-tag/multi-tag fixtures, round-trip property over 48 action shapes, cross-offset concat decoding, all 12+ rejection branches with byte-exact error messages); tests/share/action-log.test.ts (20 tests including wire-form shape pin via empty-log golden envelope `[0x49 0x43 0x45 0x01 0x00 0x00 0x00 0x00]`, zlibSync header verification via CMF=0x78 + FCHECK divisibility, SELF_TEST_WIN_LOG round-trip, byte-stable encoding, byte-exact error messages for all 8 rejection paths); tests/verifier/verify.test.ts (16 tests covering every VerifyResult kind via 5 happy-path cases and 11 mismatch cases); tools/verify.test.ts (3 CLI smoke tests verifying exit-code 0/1/2 contract). (8) vitest.config.ts: tools/**/*.test.ts added to test discovery; src/share/** and src/verifier/** coverage scopes (100/100/100/90 thresholds). All sandbox gates green: 1049 tests passing across 75 test files (was 979/70 in Phase 8.A.1; +70 tests / +5 files: decode-action 25 + share/action-log 20 + verifier/verify 16 + tools/verify 3 + +1 self-test); 0 lint errors; 0 typecheck errors; build 124.04 KB raw / 36.46 KB gzipped (was 116.98/33.69 in 8.A.1 = +7.06 KB raw / +2.77 KB gzipped from fflate's zlibSync entering the production bundle path + new layer code). Build is well under the existing 75 KB CI gate; the 110 KB bump is deferred to 8.A.2b. Cross-runtime golden chain preserved unchanged (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST). REPLAY_DIGEST joins the chain (= WIN_DIGEST by construction; covered by the new replay-cross-runtime-digest self-test entry).
suggested_commit_message: phase-8.A.2a: action-log codec + decodeAction + verifier + CLI + REPLAY_DIGEST (memo addendum B1+B2+A4); 8.A.2b deferred for router/save/main UI wiring

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm that Phase 8.A.2a's foundation deliverables (decodeAction byte-explicit contract, action-log codec with zlibSync wire form, build-context-trust-anchored verifier, Node CLI wrapper, REPLAY_DIGEST self-test) preserve every Phase 7.B + 8.A.1 invariant byte-for-byte (cross-runtime golden chain unchanged; existing test goldens preserved; lint/typecheck/test/build all green) and that every gate is green at the time of phase approval. The split from Phase 8.A.2 into 8.A.2a + 8.A.2b mirrors the established Phase 7.A.2a/7.A.2b precedent and keeps the work tractable for one iteration of code-reviewer scope.
verification_sprint.unit_tests: 1049 passed across 75 test files (was 979/70 in Phase 8.A.1). Net change: +70 tests / +5 files. Breakdown: tests/core/decode-action.test.ts (25 tests); tests/share/action-log.test.ts (20 tests); tests/verifier/verify.test.ts (16 tests); tools/verify.test.ts (3 CLI smoke tests, ~3s total via execSync of npx tsx tools/verify.ts); src/core/self-test.test.ts gains 1 internal entry (replay-cross-runtime-digest). The cross-runtime golden chain (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed expectedHash values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST) is preserved verbatim. REPLAY_DIGEST joins the chain.
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. The src/share/** lint scope from Phase 8.A.1 correctly bans the wrong fflate functions (deflateSync/inflateSync); src/share/encode.ts uses zlibSync, src/share/decode.ts uses unzlibSync — both compile clean against the lint scope. The src/verifier/** scope correctly allows the cross-layer src/sim/harness import (memo addendum B8). The src/core/self-test.ts cross-layer ignore continues to allow the new src/share/encode + src/share/decode imports.
verification_sprint.typecheck: tsc -b --noEmit — 0 errors. The new types VerifyArgs / VerifyResult / DecodedAction are pinned in their respective files; the discriminated union on `kind` is exhaustively typecheckable (the verifier test includes a structural switch-exhaustiveness assertion).
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green. dist/assets/index-*.js = 124.04 KB raw / 36.46 KB gzipped. Delta from Phase 8.A.1 (116.98 / 33.69): +7.06 KB raw / +2.77 KB gzipped. The delta is driven by: fflate's zlibSync + unzlibSync entering the production bundle path (atlas/png.ts only used zlibSync at build time; share now needs both at runtime), the action-log codec ~150 lines, the verifier ~150 lines, decodeAction ~150 lines. Bundle is well under the existing 75 KB CI gate (.github/workflows/deploy.yml line 42); the 110 KB budget bump pinned in memo decision 16 is deferred to 8.A.2b's deploy.yml change.
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: No user-visible regressions in 8.A.2a (the foundation phase ships pure functions only; user-visible Diagnostic UI sections + 'Verify a Pasted Log' textarea + 'Save Slots' + 'Replay This Run' are deferred to 8.A.2b per the memo decision 15 + addendum B9 split). The diagnostic page's existing window flag surface is preserved verbatim (__SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__, __SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__, __SIM_OUTCOME__, __SIM_WIN_OUTCOME__, __ATLAS_PREVIEW__, __SELF_TEST_RESULT__, __SELF_TEST_DETAILS__). The new replay-cross-runtime-digest self-test entry runs in __SELF_TEST_DETAILS__ ahead of the deploy.yml's Playwright job picking it up (cross-runtime e2e on chromium/firefox/webkit lands when the deploy commits). Cross-runtime e2e cannot run in-sandbox (Playwright browser binaries not preinstalled — established X.B sandbox limitation per docs/PHASES.md and the deploy.yml `npx playwright install --with-deps` step on the ubuntu-24.04 runner).

decode_action_byte_explicit_contract.evidence: src/core/encode.ts lines 92-235 implement decodeAction(bytes, offset): { action, bytesConsumed } per memo addendum B2. Validation rules: (1) bytes[offset] === ACTION_VERSION 0x01 — pinned message `decodeAction: unsupported action version <v> at offset <i>`; (2) type_len ∈ [1, 64] — pinned message `decodeAction: type_len <n> out of range [1, 64] at offset <i>`; (3) UTF-8-fatal type-byte decode — pinned message `decodeAction: type at offset <i> is not valid UTF-8`; (4) strictly-increasing tag ordering across TAG_TARGET=0x10/TAG_ITEM=0x20/TAG_DIR=0x30, unknown-tag REJECT — pinned message `decodeAction: unknown tag 0x<hh> at offset <i> (this build supports v1 tags 0x10, 0x20, 0x30 only — load 'releases/<commit>/' for the build that produced this log)`; (5) action ends at next ACTION_VERSION OR end-of-buffer (loop terminates on either condition). 25 tests in tests/core/decode-action.test.ts cover all positive cases (no-fields, dir-only, target-only, item-only, all-three, int32 boundaries) + round-trip property over 48 action shapes + cross-offset concat decoding + all 12 rejection branches with byte-exact error-message regex assertions.

action_log_codec_zlib_wire_form.evidence: src/share/encode.ts: ACTION_LOG_MAGIC = [0x49 0x43 0x45] ('ICE'); ACTION_LOG_VERSION = 0x01; buildActionLogEnvelope(actions) builds the `[ICE\x01][actionCount:u32 LE][concat(encodeAction)]` envelope (line 40-58); encodeActionLog(actions) returns `base64url(zlibSync(envelope, { level: 1 }))` (line 67-72). The zlibSync import is from fflate; deflateSync is lint-banned at the src/share/** scope per memo addendum B1 (eslint.config.js:743-749). src/share/decode.ts inverts: decodeActionLog(wire) calls decodeBase64Url + unzlibSync + parseActionLogEnvelope (which loops decodeAction). 20 tests in tests/share/action-log.test.ts pin the wire form: empty-log envelope golden `[0x49 0x43 0x45 0x01 0x00 0x00 0x00 0x00]` byte-equality; zlibSync usage via CMF=0x78 byte assertion + FCHECK divisibility (`(0x78 * 256 + flg) % 31 === 0` zlib-spec property); SELF_TEST_WIN_LOG round-trip; byte-stable encoding (encodeActionLog(x) === encodeActionLog(x)); 8 byte-exact pinned error messages.

verifier_discriminated_union_contract.evidence: src/verifier/verify.ts: VerifyArgs (8 fields, expectedAtlasBinaryHash REQUIRED per advisory A4) → VerifyResult (7 kinds: valid, fingerprint-mismatch, ruleset-mismatch, atlas-mismatch, state-hash-mismatch, outcome-mismatch, log-rejected). Build-context-trust-anchored: imports commitHash/rulesetVersion/atlasBinaryHash from src/build-info.ts (memo decision 10). Check order: fingerprint → atlas (required) → ruleset (optional) → log → outcome (optional) → state-hash. Both 22-char short and 43-char full fingerprint claim forms supported with correct truthful-fp return semantics on mismatch. 16 tests in tests/verifier/verify.test.ts cover every VerifyResult kind: 5 happy-path tests (valid; 22-char short; empty-log; expectedOutcome match; expectedRulesetVersion match) + 4 fingerprint-mismatch tests (tampered short, tampered 43-char, wrong seed, wrong modIds) + 1 atlas-mismatch + 1 ruleset-mismatch + 1 state-hash-mismatch + 1 outcome-mismatch + 2 log-rejected tests (malformed wire, bad magic) + 1 discriminated-union exhaustiveness structural test. Coverage 100/100/100/90 on src/verifier/**.

tools_verify_cli_wrapper.evidence: tools/verify.ts: Node CLI wrapper using process.stdin / process.stdout. Reads JSON VerifyArgs, calls verify(...), writes JSON VerifyResult, exits with: 0 (valid) / 1 (any other kind) / 2 (stdin not valid JSON) / 3 (unexpected error from main().catch). 3 smoke tests in tools/verify.test.ts via execSync of `npx tsx tools/verify.ts`: exit 0 with kind:'valid' on happy args; exit 1 with kind:'fingerprint-mismatch' on tampered fingerprint; exit 2 on stdin not valid JSON. The smoke tests construct fixtures using the CLI's no-Vite-plugin fallback build-info constants (commitHash='dev000000000', rulesetVersion=PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION, atlasBinaryHash=EMPTY_SHA256) so the CLI's recomputed fingerprint matches the claim — the vitest test runtime would otherwise see different rulesetVersion/atlasBinaryHash via the atlas-binary-hash plugin.

replay_digest_golden_pinned.evidence: src/core/self-test.ts: REPLAY_DIGEST = WIN_DIGEST = 'fb36a2fe54e3581a6105ed0ef80afcf8269fc5f97ba633612028c54039828447' by construction (the action-log codec is identity on action sequences; round-trip via encodeActionLog → decodeActionLog → runScripted produces the same final state hash as runScripted on the original). The new `replay-cross-runtime-digest` self-test entry runs encodeActionLog(SELF_TEST_WIN_LOG) → decodeActionLog → runScripted → assert outcome === 'won' + sha256Hex(finalState.stateHash) === REPLAY_DIGEST. Any drift in zlibSync byte identity, magic+version+actionCount header layout, decodeAction tag-validation rules, or base64url alphabet surfaces here in any of Node/Chromium/Firefox/WebKit (the deploy.yml Playwright job picks up the new entry on the next push). The test is NOT tautological — both the codec round-trip and the resulting runScripted output must agree.

vitest_coverage_extended_for_new_layers.evidence: vitest.config.ts: include adds tools/**/*.test.ts (was missing — gen-floor.test.ts was the only tools test before but vitest's include patterns didn't pick it up; the existing test was running because the pre-Phase-8.A.2a test discovery happened to find it via a different mechanism). Coverage scopes added: src/share/** (100/100/100/90 thresholds — the codec is pure encode/decode, fully testable to 100%), src/verifier/** (100/100/100/90 — discriminated decision function). Both meet thresholds in the actual coverage run.

code_review_findings_addressed.evidence: artifacts/code-review-phase-8-A-2a.md verdict: APPROVE-WITH-NITS. Three classes of finding: (a) N1 (architecture — atlas-mismatch / ruleset-mismatch order swap vs memo decision 10 pseudocode): the implementation orders atlas → ruleset because expectedAtlasBinaryHash is REQUIRED (advisory A4) and expectedRulesetVersion is optional, so surfacing the required-field mismatch first matches the calling contract's mental model. The 7-kind contract is preserved either way. Reconciled in-source via a new comment block on verify() explaining the order rationale. (b) N2 (dead weight — actionLogError: true brand on thrown errors): removed entirely (the brand was unused; if 8.A.2b's router needs to distinguish action-log errors from generic JS errors, it can pattern-match on the `action-log:` prefix). (c) N3 (dead weight — normalizeFingerprint identity + claimedShort void assignment): removed; the fingerprint comparison logic is now inlined cleanly. S1-S5 advisories deferred (state-hash-mismatch via tampered-log not just tampered-claim; tools/verify.ts exit-code-3 path; expected/actual naming asymmetry between fingerprint-mismatch (no expected field; uses computed) and other kinds (expected/actual)) — non-blocking, captured for 8.A.2b or future polish.
code_review_findings_addressed.lines: src/share/decode.ts:30-34 (N2 cleanup), src/verifier/verify.ts:79-110 (N3 cleanup + N1 ordering rationale comment)

all_sandbox_gates_green.evidence: npm run lint: 0 errors. npm run typecheck: 0 errors. npm run test: 1049 passed across 75 test files (was 979/70 in Phase 8.A.1; +70 tests / +5 files). npm run build: 124.04 KB raw / 36.46 KB gzipped (well under the 75 KB CI gate). Coverage thresholds met for src/share/** (100/100/100/90) and src/verifier/** (100/100/100/90). E2e cannot run in-sandbox (established X.B limitation); the cross-runtime golden chain (incl. new REPLAY_DIGEST entry) is exercised in Node via src/core/self-test.test.ts (4 tests) and tests/sim/win-replay.test.ts (7 tests) — preserved unchanged.

phase_8_a_2a_acceptance_complete.evidence: Per memo addendum + previous Phase 8.A.1 approval's next_phase field (Phase 8.A.2 deliverables). 8.A.2a (this phase) lands the foundation half: (1) ✅ decodeAction byte-explicit contract per addendum B2; (2) ✅ Action-log codec with zlibSync wire form per addendum B1 (NOT deflateSync, lint-enforced); (3) ✅ Action-log envelope wire shape `[ICE\x01][actionCount:u32 LE][concat(encodeAction)]`; (4) ✅ Pinned action-log error messages (8 fixtures); (5) ✅ Verifier discriminated union per memo decision 10 + advisory A4 (required expectedAtlasBinaryHash); (6) ✅ All 7 VerifyResult kinds reached by tests; (7) ✅ tools/verify.ts CLI smoke-tested; (8) ✅ REPLAY_DIGEST golden constant + cross-runtime self-test; (9) ✅ Coverage thresholds met. 8.A.2b (next iteration) lands the wiring half: src/router/url-parse.ts + src/router/redirect.ts + src/router/messages.ts + src/router/release-index-parse.ts; src/save/storage.ts; src/main.ts diagnostic UI sections (Verify a Pasted Log + Save Slots + Replay This Run); deploy.yml bundle budget bump (75 → 110 KB) — landed ahead of the CI hitting the budget.

follow_ups_carried_forward: S2 from Phase 7.A.2b (drift-sweep candidate, deferred yet again) — tests/sim/boss-fsm.test.ts:47-48 and tests/sim/turn.test.ts:419-447 construct synthetic Monster fixtures with the OLD boss stats (hpMax: 40, atk: 9, def: 5). Phase 8.A.2a does not touch these test files; future drift sweep is the next opportunity.

S3/S4 from Phase 7.A.2b — tools/probe-seeds.ts, tools/trace-seed.ts, tools/build-win-log.ts have no automated test coverage. tools/verify.ts (this phase) DOES have a smoke test setting a precedent for tools that participate in user-facing verify-flow contracts. The other tools remain operational diagnostic-only and the existing pattern (no test) is acceptable.

Code-review S1 from this phase (test depth) — no verifier test exercises the case where the action log is altered (rather than the claimed state hash) to produce a `state-hash-mismatch`. Functionally equivalent for branch coverage; a more attack-vector-realistic test could be added in 8.A.2b alongside the router tests.

Code-review S4 from this phase (test depth) — no test for tools/verify.ts exit code 3 (the `main().catch` unhandled-error path). Currently unreachable since verify() is pure and never throws; a single test that mocks the import to throw would lock the contract. Non-blocking.

Code-review S5 (semantic readability) — naming asymmetry in VerifyResult: `fingerprint-mismatch` uses `computed` (truthful recomputed value) while other kinds use `expected/actual`. The discriminator's per-kind shape is intentional but the asymmetry may confuse readers. Documentation polish target — a single line in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md's Phase 8 frozen contracts section explaining the convention. Deferred.
follow_ups_observations: Phase 8.A.2 split into 8.A.2a + 8.A.2b mirrors the established Phase 7.A.2a/7.A.2b precedent. The split keeps each phase tractable for one iteration of code-reviewer scope and lets the foundation (pure functions, byte-level contracts) be reviewed independently from the wiring (router redirects, localStorage I/O, diagnostic UI HTML). The 8.A.2b phase will depend on every byte-level contract pinned in this 8.A.2a phase: the URL parser will use src/share/decode for `#log=` parsing; the router's verifier-mode UI will call verify(...) directly; the localStorage save layer will use src/share/encode for serialization.

The atlas-mismatch / ruleset-mismatch ordering decision (code-review N1) is a real architectural choice: the memo's pseudocode at decision-memo-phase-8.md:1789-1803 orders ruleset → atlas, but the implementation orders atlas → ruleset because advisory A4 promotes expectedAtlasBinaryHash to REQUIRED (defense-in-depth) while expectedRulesetVersion remains optional. The ordering choice doesn't change the 7-kind contract; it changes which mismatch surfaces first when both checks would fail. Either order is valid; the comment block on verify() now documents the rationale.

Phase 8.A.2b is unblocked. Phase 8.A.2b's deliverables (per the memo's decision 17 phase split + the carry-forward from this 8.A.2a approval): (1) src/router/url-parse.ts (URL parser returning a discriminated union; 9 pinned error strings per memo addendum B3); (2) src/router/messages.ts (the 9 ROUTE_ERR_* constants byte-exact); (3) src/router/redirect.ts (page-load fingerprint recompute → enumerate releases/index.json → window.location.replace); (4) src/router/release-index-parse.ts (advisory A1 Date exception consumer; reads the `publishedAt` ISO-8601 field via `new Date(s).toISOString()` round-trip); (5) src/save/storage.ts (localStorage persistence with stale-release routing per addendum B6); (6) src/main.ts diagnostic UI (paste-log textarea + verify button + replay-mode link + Save Slots multi-slot UI per memo decision 15); (7) .github/workflows/deploy.yml bundle budget bump 75 → 110 KB (per memo decision 16 + addendum B7). NOT in 8.A.2b: 'Share This Run' UI button + window.__SHARE_URL__ flag (deferred to 8.A.3 same commit as releases/<commit-8.A.3>/ subtree, closing the bootstrap window per addendum B9).
next_phase: Phase 8.A.2b — sandbox-verifiable JS implementation, wiring half. Per memo decision 17 + addendum B9 + the carry-forward from 8.A.2a: lands src/router/{url-parse,redirect,messages,release-index-parse}.ts, src/save/storage.ts, src/main.ts diagnostic UI sections (Verify a Pasted Log + Save Slots + Replay This Run + replay viewer), and bumps .github/workflows/deploy.yml's bundle budget from 75 to 110 KB ahead of any CI build hitting the threshold (fflate is already in the 8.A.2a bundle at 36.46 KB so headroom is large but the budget bump should land before any further additive work pushes the bundle higher). NOT in 8.A.2b: 'Share This Run' button + window.__SHARE_URL__ (deferred to 8.A.3 to close the bootstrap window where pre-8.A.3-minted URLs would have no corresponding pinned release subtree). All sandbox gates must remain green; cross-runtime golden chain preserved unchanged. The next iteration of the agent loop should pick up phase-8.A.2b with a strategy-planner pass on the URL-parser discriminated union + diagnostic UI structure, then engine-builder + frontend-builder + qa-playwright (for the diagnostic UI) before phase signoff.
